title: Inheritance
description: Learn to inherit properties and methods from base classes, use access control specifiers, and avoid multiple inheritance traps.
learningObjectives: Derive classes from base classes using public inheritance
Override properties while calling base implementation methods
Avoid multiple inheritance traps using composition paradigms

sections.title: Extending Blueprints and Hierarchies
sections.content:
Inheritance enables a derived class to acquire members of a base class, expressing an 'is-a' relationship. In game engines, this is how specialized classes (e.g. `APawn` -> `ACharacter` -> `APlayerCharacter`) build on generic engine subsystems.

While C++ supports multiple inheritance (inheriting from more than one class), this often introduces complexity (like the Diamond Problem, where a class inherits from two classes that share a common base). Game programmers prefer composition ('has-a') or single-inheritance hierarchies for clarity and cache safety.

sections.title: Base Class Method Calls
